issues.author: shakeebshaan

previousFix.description: Root cause of '[16] Account reauth failed': on Android, @capgo/capacitor-social-login routes through Credential Manager, which defaults filterByAuthorizedAccounts:true — it ONLY offers Google accounts that have ALREADY authorized this app. A first-time user (or a Family-Link / freshly-added account) has no authorized account, so Credential Manager returns NO credential and the SDK reports [16] reauth failed instead of showing a picker. Fix (v134, src/lib/google-auth.ts): force the bottom-sheet picker over ALL accounts on login -> options { style:'bottom', filterByAuthorizedAccounts:false, autoSelectEnabled:false }. This lets a brand-new user pick any Google account and authorize for the first time. tsc clean; shipped in versionCode 134 (1.6.76), built + uploaded to Play alpha. NOTE: native Google auth can't be reproduced in headless Playwright QA (proof image = clean Welcome render, build healthy, button present), so this needs a quick on-device test. If [16] still appears after v134 on a device, the remaining cause is Google Cloud Console OAuth config (the Android app's SHA-1 / package name not registered on the OAuth client) — that's owner-side; flag back and I'll walk through it.

judge.note: Code fix is solid + tsc-clean and the build renders cleanly (0 console errors), but the native Google account-picker cannot be exercised in headless QA, so on-device confirmation is still owner-side.

issues.reviewReason:
Root-caused the persistent "[16] Account reauth failed" — it is NOT a code bug; it is a Google Cloud / Play Console config blocker that only you can fix (I have no Console access). Evidence: I verified the app code is correct — it passes the WEB OAuth client (…d8ktncq…apps.googleusercontent.com) as the serverClientId, package is com.nyus.nyusand. Both Android sign-in paths the plugin supports (one-tap bottom-sheet AND the standard "Sign in with Google" chooser) return the SAME [16], and [16] appeared even before my v134 change — that only happens when Credential Manager cannot validate the installed app against the OAuth client, i.e. a SIGNING-FINGERPRINT or CONSENT-SCREEN registration problem. Alpha builds are signed by Google Play App Signing (Play re-signs), so the SHA-1 that must be registered is the PLAY signing cert's — not the upload key's.

FIX (Google Cloud Console + Play Console, ~3 min):
1) Play Console -> your app -> Test and release -> Setup -> App integrity -> "App signing key certificate" -> copy its SHA-1 fingerprint.
2) Google Cloud Console (project 415757416413) -> APIs & Services -> Credentials -> OAuth 2.0 Client IDs -> the ANDROID client (…veopshgfin…) -> add that Play App Signing SHA-1. The client currently has only a5:83:5c:43:22:81…; the upload key is b9:5c:ea:c4:08:94:17:5b:7e:e1:e8:91:aa:d1:2e:22:18:4a:04:4c. Add the Play signing SHA-1 (and you can add the upload-key one too for local release builds).
3) OAuth consent screen -> Publishing status must be "In production" (email/profile/openid need no Google verification). If it is in "Testing", either publish it OR add your test Google account under Test users — otherwise non-listed accounts get reauth failures.
4) If google-services.json changed, re-download it into android/app/ and I will rebuild.

Once you confirm (1)-(3) are done, reopen/comment and I will rebuild + you re-test. Optional code tweak I can also ship: switch the in-app flow from the v134 one-tap bottom-sheet back to the standard "Sign in with Google" chooser (better for first-time users) — but it will NOT resolve [16] until the SHA-1/consent above is fixed.
